generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox assembly in the robot's first axis drive train, transmitting rotational torque from motor to output shaft"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft for real-time angular position measurement of the motor"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngleData
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder positioned behind the cycloidal gearbox to measure gearbox output angle, reducing backlash effects"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxAngleData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring electrical input current (I_M) to the permanent magnet synchronous motor"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance TorqueDerivationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngleData
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngleData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance StructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) defining the motor-gearbox-cycloidal assembly composition with rotational torque/velocity flow definitions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink model simulating ideal gearbox behavior without friction components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction dynamics for motor and gearbox components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance TorqueCalculationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model using manufacturer-specific parameters to derive torque (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) from motor current measurements"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SiemensSINUMERIK840D
]

instance AngularVelocityCalculationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model computing angular velocity (ω) from encoder measurements and system kinematics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SiemensSINUMERIK840D
]

instance SimulatedDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Outputs from friction models (frictionless and friction-included) for comparison with measured data traces"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData, GearboxAngleData, TorqueDerivationData, AngularVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance MeasuredDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time measured data including input current (I_M), output angle, torque (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out), and angular velocity (ω) from encoders and dynamics calculations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData, MotorAngleData, GearboxAngleData, TorqueDerivationData, AngularVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocol
]

instance MetadataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Source attribution (motor encoder, gearbox encoder) and computational origins (torque derivation from current) for traceability"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData, TorqueDerivationData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomDashboard
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling environment used for creating structural models (BDD/IBD) of the robot's first axis assembly"
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModel
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink platform for executable simulation models of friction dynamics and system behavior"
    DTDFVocab:enables IdealGearboxModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time sensor data streaming from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller"
    DTDFVocab:enables MeasuredDataTrace
]

instance CustomDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom visualization dashboard aggregating data traces (I_M, ω, τ) for friction analysis and anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:enables MeasuredDataTrace, SimulatedDataTrace, MetadataTrace
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840D : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller providing position/speed/torque control and dynamics calculations"
    DTDFVocab:enables TorqueCalculationModel, AngularVelocityCalculationModel
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service combining white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, LuGre) for real-time parameter refinement"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossInsight, FrictionParameterUpdate
]

instance MultiPhysicsAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous identification of friction losses by comparing simulated (frictionless/friction-included) and measured data traces across motor, gearwheel, and system"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionDeviationInsight, SystemDynamicsInsight
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic updates to friction models enabling adaptive robot control for improved motion accuracy in machining tasks"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterOptimization
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregated data trace visualization dashboard displaying input current, torque, angular velocity alongside simulated outputs for intuitive comparison"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetection, FrictionTrendAnalysis
]

instance ModelExchangePipeline : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Facilitates dynamic updates of friction models during operation for continuous improvement of robot motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelUpdateAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionLossInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of friction losses (static, kinetic, viscous) via comparison of simulated and measured data traces (torque/velocity deviations)"
]

instance FrictionParameterUpdate : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic refinement of friction model parameters based on live data comparison to optimize control performance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of friction-related deviations in system dynamics between simulated and measured data traces"
]

instance SystemDynamicsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous analysis of overall system dynamics to detect cumulative friction effects across motor-gearbox assembly"
]

instance ControlParameterOptimization : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive adjustment of control parameters to compensate for identified friction losses in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetection : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Operator visualization of data anomalies or unexpected friction trends through aggregated trace comparisons"
]

instance FrictionTrendAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Longitudinal analysis of friction behavior patterns over operational cycles for predictive maintenance"
]

instance ModelUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated deployment of updated friction models to the digital twin during operation based on performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C17: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxActuator,
                MotorEncoder, GearboxEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor,
                StructuralModel, IdealGearboxModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel,
                TorqueCalculationModel, AngularVelocityCalculationModel,
                MeasuredDataTrace, SimulatedDataTrace, MetadataTrace
]

instance OperationalControlEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SiemensSINUMERIK840D, MQTTProtocol, CustomDashboard,
                FrictionModelingService, MultiPhysicsAnalysisService,
                AdaptiveControlService, DataVisualizationService,
                ModelExchangePipeline
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and near-real-time**:
  - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, encoders) is streamed continuously via MQTT, enabling **low-latency processing** (sub-second updates).
  - **Model refinement cycle**: Friction models are continuously updated during operation, with adjustments triggered by deviations between simulated and measured data (e.g., via live data comparison in the dashboard).
  - **Synchronization rate**: Aligned with the robot’s operational speed (up to 75°/s), ensuring timely feedback for control adjustments."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is **asset-centric and purpose-driven**, focusing on the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot. It integrates:
  - **Asset Model**: SysML-based structural and behavioral models of the motor, gearbox, and cycloidal gear.
  - **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, motor current) and virtual models (Simulink simulations) for friction analysis.
  - **Processing Pipeline**: A **Processing component** (incarnated as Simulation classes in the digital shadow) that consumes input data traces (e.g., I_M, ω) and produces output traces (e.g., simulated torque/velocity) for friction identification.
  - **Dashboard Integration**: Unified visualization of measured vs. simulated data to support real-time decision-making."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates across the following life-cycle stages:
  - **As-designed stage**: Structural and behavioral models (SysML Block Definition Diagram, Internal Block Diagram) describe the robot’s first axis architecture, including components (motor, gearbox) and flow definitions (rotational torque/velocity, electrical current).
  - **As-manufactured stage**: Real-time sensor data and live measurements (e.g., motor current, encoders) are integrated into the digital shadow to validate and refine friction models.
  - **As-operated stage**: Continuous friction monitoring and model updates occur during robot operation, enabling adaptive control for improved accuracy in machining tasks."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Model Accuracy**: White-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models are combined to capture static, kinetic, and viscous effects, with parameters adjusted dynamically via live data.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: Traceability of data origins (e.g., sensor vs. simulation) ensures validation of model outputs against physical measurements.
  - **Verification/Validation**:
    - **SysML Models**: Structurally validated via component interconnection diagrams (BDD, IBD).
    - **Simulink Models**: Physically validated by comparing simulated vs. measured data traces (e.g., torque/velocity discrepancies).
  - **Error Sources**: Backlash reduction via encoder pair analysis, torque derivation from current using manufacturer-specific constants."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s technical connection includes:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for real-time sensor data streaming from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller.
  - **Data Flow**: One-way data flow from physical sensors to the digital shadow (passive digital shadow), with active processing components (e.g., Simulink simulations) interpreting data for friction analysis.
  - **Integration Architecture**: Asset Administration Shell (AAS) principles are implied for structured data representation, though not explicitly detailed in the documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to:
  - **Modeling Standards**: **SysML** for structural/behavioral modeling of the robot’s first axis.
  - **Simulation Standards**: **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for executable friction models.
  - **Data Standards**: MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data exchange, though no explicit mention of industry-specific standards (e.g., OPC UA) for digital twin integration."
]
